title: Install GTM MarTech Plugin
description: Integrate Google Analytics 4 and Google Tag Manager with your AEM Edge Delivery Services project using a phase-based loading approach optimized for Core Web Vitals.

tasks.title: Verify Google Analytics & Tag Manager prerequisites
tasks.description:
Before configuring the plugin, ensure you have access to and have preconfigured the following:

**Required access:**
- Google Analytics
- Google Tag Manager

**Required preconfiguration:**
- A data stream in Google Analytics
- A Google Tag Manager Workspace

**Legal disclaimer:** This library defaults user consent to `denied`. Setting user consent to `granted` overrides this behavior to grant consent by default (i.e. without explicit end user agreement). Consult with your own legal counsel to understand your privacy obligations and the appropriate use and configuration of this library. We also recommend using a consent management system.

tasks.title: Add preload hints to head.html
tasks.description:
Add the following three lines at the **end** of `head.html`:
- `<script nonce="aem" src="/scripts/gtm-martech.js" type="module"></script>`
- `<link rel="preload" as="script" crossorigin="anonymous" href="/plugins/gtm-martech/src/index.js"/>`
- `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://www.googletagmanager.com"/>`

tasks.title: Create GTM MarTech helper script
tasks.description:
Create `scripts/gtm-martech.js` with the following:
- Import `GtmMartech` from `../plugins/gtm-martech/src/index.js`
- Add the DA Preview disable check: `const disabled = window.location.search.includes('martech=off');`
- Instantiate `new GtmMartech(config)` with:
  - `analytics: !disabled`
  - `tags: []` (empty — user fills in GA4 IDs)
  - `containers: { lazy: [], delayed: [] }` (empty — user fills in GTM IDs)
  - `consent: !disabled`
  - `consentCallback: undefined` (placeholder)
  - `decorateCallback: undefined` (placeholder)
- Export the instance as `default`
- Include `TODO` comments where the user must fill in their GA4/GTM IDs and callbacks

tasks.title: Import GTM MarTech in scripts.js
tasks.description:
Add the following import at the **top** of `scripts/scripts.js` with the other import statements:

`import gtmMartech from './gtm-martech.js';`

tasks.title: Wire eager phase into loadEager
tasks.description:
In the `loadEager` function in `scripts/scripts.js`:
- Add `gtmMartech.eager()` to the existing `Promise.all()` call alongside `loadSection()`
- This initializes GA4 scripts during the eager rendering phase
- The call is async and should be awaited via `Promise.all`

tasks.title: Wire lazy phase into loadLazy
tasks.description:
In the `loadLazy` function in `scripts/scripts.js`:
- Add `await gtmMartech.lazy();` **after** `loadSections(main)` completes
- **CRITICAL:** This must **NOT** be inside a `Promise.all()` — it must be awaited independently to ensure correct section/block decoration

tasks.title: Wire delayed phase into loadDelayed
tasks.description:
In the `loadDelayed` function in `scripts/scripts.js`:
- Add `window.setTimeout(gtmMartech.delayed, 1000);` **before** the existing `delayed.js` import
- This is only needed if the user configures delayed GTM containers

tasks.title: Configure GA4 Measurement IDs
tasks.description:
Open `scripts/gtm-martech.js` and replace the empty `tags` array with your GA4 Measurement ID(s):
- Format: `G-XXXXXXXXXX`
- Location: **Google Analytics > Admin > Data Streams > your stream > Measurement ID**
- Multiple IDs can be provided as an array

tasks.title: Configure GTM Container IDs
tasks.description:
Open `scripts/gtm-martech.js` and add your GTM Container ID(s) (format: `GTM-XXXXXXX`) to the appropriate arrays:
- **`containers.lazy`** — containers that process events (loaded after LCP)
- **`containers.delayed`** — non-critical third-party tags (loaded later)

tasks.title: Configure consent management
tasks.description:
If your site requires cookie consent, implement the `consentCallback` function in `scripts/gtm-martech.js`.

Requirements:
- Must be an async function returning an object with [Google consent types](https://developers.google.com/tag-platform/security/concepts/consent-mode#consent-types)
- Each key set to `"granted"` or `"denied"`:
  - `ad_storage`
  - `ad_user_data`
  - `ad_personalization`
  - `analytics_storage`
  - `functionality_storage`
  - `personalization_storage`
  - `security_storage`
- Integrate with your consent management platform (e.g., OneTrust, Cookiebot)

If consent is not needed, set `consent: false` in the config.

tasks.title: Implement event decoration callback
tasks.description:
Optionally implement the `decorateCallback` function in `scripts/gtm-martech.js` to add custom DataLayer pushes on sections and blocks:
- The function is called with each section/block element during the lazy phase
- Use `martech.pushToDataLayer(payload)` to send events
- Check `el.classList.contains('block')` or `el.classList.contains('section')` to differentiate element types

tasks.title: Update ESLint ignore (if applicable)
tasks.description:
If the project has an `.eslintignore` file:
- Add `plugins/gtm-martech/*` to prevent linting of the plugin source files

If no `.eslintignore` exists, skip this task.

tasks.title: Review and merge plugin branch
tasks.description:
Review all changes on the `plugin/gtm-martech` branch. Verify:
- `head.html` has the three new lines (script, preload, preconnect)
- `scripts/gtm-martech.js` exists with correct config structure
- `scripts/scripts.js` has the import and all three phase calls wired correctly
- `lazy()` is **NOT** in a `Promise.all`

Create a pull request and merge to main.
